todays_post.title: Thursday March 12 | Sales | SLAY | Familiar | CTA: B
todays_post.content:
# Thursday March 12 | Sales | SLAY | Familiar | CTA: B

![Post Image](2026-03-12-dad-story.png)

---

My dad never understood what I do.
Last week he finally did.

He's 72. Retired engineer. Practical man.

For 20 years, every time someone asked him what his son does, he'd say: "Something with computers."

He wasn't wrong. He just couldn't picture it.

Last week, he came to visit.

I showed him one screen.
Not a presentation. Not a diagram.

A live dashboard showing patient flow across 15 hospitals in 3 countries.

Real-time bed occupancy. Clinical alerts. Pending discharges. Revenue per episode.

All feeding from systems that didn't talk to each other 18 months ago.

He stared at it for a full minute.

Then he said: "So you built the nervous system."

That's it.
That's the job.

I've spent 20 years building nervous systems for organizations.

At Intel, it was the network infrastructure connecting LTE towers.
At PaySky, it was Egypt's first SuperApp connecting payments, loans, and insurance in one platform.
At Talabat, it was the operations layer that scaled from 30,000 daily orders to 7 million.
At Network International, it was the PMO connecting 300+ banking projects across 8 countries.

And now: a $50M transformation connecting 15 hospitals across 3 countries.

Every one of those jobs had a different title.
None of them had a simple answer to "what do you do?"

But my dad got it in 30 seconds.

Because the job was always the same.

**Find the broken connections. Build the ones that matter.**

The title changes. The work doesn't.

What's the clearest someone has ever described complex work back to you?

..

By the way, I've been documenting my journey managing a $50M hospital transformation across 3 countries. If you're navigating a similar challenge, happy to share what's working. Drop a comment or DM.

#Leadership #DigitalTransformation #HealthTech #Career #GCC

posts.title: Thank you Oracle! Proud of my team #OracleDigital #Dubai
posts.author: Ashkanparpinchee
posts.topic: Thank you Oracle! Proud of my team ... Embedding digital transformation within corporate strategy to drive sustainable growth and operational
posts.ready_comment: Embedding transformation at the strategy level is where most organizations still struggle, treating it as a project rather than a core operating principle. Having led a $50M digital transformation across 15 hospitals at Saudi German Hospital Group, I saw firsthand how quickly initiatives stall when they lack executive alignment from day one. The teams that win are the ones where the CTO and CFO are speaking the same language before a single line of code is written. What's been your biggest lever for keeping leadership aligned as the transformation scales?

posts.title: King Salman International Airport's Post
posts.author: King Salman International Airport
posts.topic: The CSSO discussed the future vision for empowering national talent and integrating intelligent systems in #KSIA during his participation in
posts.ready_comment: Empowering national talent while simultaneously integrating intelligent systems is one of the hardest balancing acts in large-scale infrastructure transformation. Having led a $50M digital transformation across 15 hospitals under Saudi Vision 2030, I saw firsthand how the sequencing of people development and technology adoption makes or breaks execution. The temptation to let the tech sprint ahead of the talent readiness curve is real, and the airports that get this right will define the benchmark for smart infrastructure in the region. What mechanisms is KSIA building to ensure national talent isn't just present in the room but actually driving the intelligent systems agenda?
